docs.title: orchast_agent
docs.text:
Welcome to orchast_agent, a comprehensive monorepo of purpose-built AI agents designed for real-world utility and high performance.

Built on top of the Google ADK framework, orchast_agent demonstrates the power of modular agent architecture, leveraging both cutting-edge cloud LLMs (Gemini) and flexible local models (Ollama).

docs.title: Why orchast_agent?
docs.text: The goal of this project is to provide a collection of professional, modular AI agent implementations that serve as both functional tools and reference architectures for building complex agentic workflows. Whether it's automating scientific paper writing or evolving its own toolset at runtime, orchast_agent pushes the boundaries of what autonomous agents can achieve.

docs.title: The Agent Roster
docs.text: Agent Description Primary Models Self-Evolving Agent Designs and registers its own tools at runtime with a desktop GUI. Gemini Flash, Ollama Book Writer High-throughput book generation pipeline with GitHub auto-commit. Ollama (Local) Course Generator Multilingual course creation via a 4-agent sequential pipeline. Gemini Flash Caveman Compressor Compresses verbose technical text into terse, efficient summaries. Gemini Flash Tutorial Debug Agent Interactive ADK tutorials and real-time terminal error debugging. Gemini Flash Tiny Jarvis Telegram-based scheduling agent with a multi-agent coordination team. Gemini Flash, Ollama Scientific Paper Writer Publication-ready paper generation using a 5-agent pipeline. Gemini Flash Tiny Company Multi-agent simulation building a TOPIK learning assistant web app. Gemini Flash

docs.text: New to the project? Start with our Quick-Start Guide to set up your environment and run your first agent in minutes.

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: This page provides a detailed guide on how to configure the agents in the orchast_agent monorepo, including environment variables and LLM model selection.

docs.title: Model Selection
docs.text: The agents are designed to be flexible, supporting both high-performance cloud models and privacy-focused local models via the LiteLlm abstraction layer.

docs.title: Gemini Flash (Cloud)
docs.text: Default Choice. Best for most users due to its high reliability in tool calling and rapid response times. - Requirement: A Google Cloud Project with the Vertex AI API enabled. - Authentication: Handled via Application Default Credentials (ADC). bash gcloud auth application-default login

docs.title: Ollama (Local)
docs.text: Ideal for offline development, privacy-sensitive data, or avoiding cloud costs. - Requirement: Ollama installed and running on your local machine or a remote GPU server. - Connectivity: Agents connect to the OpenAI-compatible endpoint at http://localhost:11434/v1.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Depending on the agent, you can override default behaviors using environment variables.

Variable Description Example / Default Required AGENT_MODEL Specifies the model to be used by the agent. If set, it typically triggers local LLM mode (Ollama). qwen3:32b, gemma4:31b No GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT Your GCP project ID for Vertex AI requests. my-ai-project-123 Yes (for Gemini) OLLAMA_HOST The host address of your Ollama server (if not localhost). http://192.168.1.50:11434 No

docs.title: Example: Running with a Local Model
docs.text:
To run an agent using a specific Ollama model, prefix the execution command:

AGENT_MODEL=qwen3:32b agents-cli playground

docs.title: Ollama Setup Guide
docs.text: If you choose to use local models, follow these steps to prepare your environment:

docs.title: 1. Install Ollama
docs.text: Download and install from ollama.com.

docs.title: 2. Pull a Compatible Model
docs.text:
Not all models are equal in their ability to perform tool-calling (function calling). We recommend the following:

# Highly recommended for tool creation/execution
ollama pull qwen3:32b

# Alternative option
ollama pull gemma4:31b

docs.title: 3. Verify Installation
docs.text:
Ensure Ollama is running by visiting http://localhost:11434 in your browser or running:

ollama list

docs.title: Troubleshooting Configuration
docs.text:
• Authentication Errors: If you see "Permission Denied" when using Gemini, ensure you have run gcloud auth application-default login and that the quota for Vertex AI is active.
  • Model Loops: Some local models may enter a loop of calling the same tool repeatedly. The agents include a _limit_tool_loops callback to prevent this, but switching to a larger model (e.g., 32B+) usually resolves the issue.
  • Port Conflicts: If the playground UI fails to start on port 8000, you can specify a different port: bash agents-cli playground --port 8080

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to orchast_agent!

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository.
  2. Create a feature branch.
  3. Submit a Pull Request.

docs.title: Contribution Guidelines
docs.text:
• Follow the project's coding standards.
  • Ensure tests pass before submitting.
  • Provide clear descriptions of changes.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you set up your environment and get your first AI agent from the orchast_agent monorepo running locally.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure you have the following tools installed on your system:

Requirement Version/Link Installation Command / Note Python 3.11+ python.org uv Latest curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh \| sh Google Cloud SDK Latest Install gcloud CLI Ollama Latest ollama.com (Required for local agents) Agents-CLI Latest uv tool install google-agents-cli

docs.title: 1. Authentication
docs.text:
If you plan to use Gemini (Cloud LLMs), you must authenticate your Google Cloud environment:

gcloud auth application-default login
gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID

docs.title: 2. Choose Your Agent
docs.text:
Navigate into the directory of the agent you wish to run. For example, to try the Self-Evolving Agent:

cd self-evolving-agent

docs.title: 3. Install and Run
docs.text:
The uv package manager is used across this monorepo for fast, reproducible environments.

# Sync dependencies
uv sync

# Start the agent (e.g., opening the playground UI)
agents-cli playground

The browser UI will typically be available at http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: Local Development Workflow
docs.text: Each agent is independent. To contribute or modify an agent: 1. Enter the agent's specific directory. 2. Review its local README.md for agent-specific configurations. 3. Use uv run <script>.py to execute logic directly.

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This page provides examples and common workflows for using orchast_agent.

docs.title: Basic Example
docs.text: python main.py --input "hello world"
